claim: Observable expert workflow anchors improved cross-LLM judge agreement relative to generic rubrics
scope: 92 Figma runs reported in rubric comparison
caveat: Comparison changed author source rubric count grouping weights anchor language and observability together
decision_use: Support for piloting the combined expert-rubric package; not evidence that skill grounding alone caused the difference

claim: Generic rubrics had lower reported inter-judge agreement
caveat: Does not isolate expert authorship as the cause
decision_use: Comparator evidence supporting a pilot while limiting attribution

claim: Expert workflow anchors reduced reported judge variance
decision_use: Support for reliability testing; cannot establish validity or adoption readiness

claim: Generic rubrics had higher reported judge variance
decision_use: Comparator evidence supporting reliability testing; not professional-validity evidence

claim: Fine-grained automated output grades weakly matched human judgments
caveat: One Figma domain expert limits human-baseline generality
decision_use: Disconfirming evidence against immediate adoption; motivates independent expert validation

claim: Fine-grained automated skill grades weakly matched human judgments
caveat: No independent human process annotation validates the skill judges
decision_use: Disconfirming evidence against immediate adoption; motivates blinded process annotation

claim: Skill effects were directional and heterogeneous
scope: Claude Codex and Gemini harness/model systems
caveat: Sample is underpowered and harness/model/integration factors remain bundled
decision_use: Insufficient causal evidence; motivates a controlled factorial or blocked paired pilot

claim: Large reported directional gain for one configured system
scope: Subset of the 7 paired Figma runs
caveat: Too few runs for a stable causal adoption claim
decision_use: Promising signal for a pilot; insufficient for adoption

claim: Public procedure may improve completion for some systems
caveat: Deployment difference alone cannot separate procedure content from integration and stochasticity
decision_use: Exploratory operational signal; motivates controlled completion and integration measurements

claim: Relative preference does not imply absolute readiness
scope: 31 rated Figma tasks with one domain expert
caveat: Single-expert threshold is not a universal professional standard
decision_use: Disconfirming evidence against treating relative wins as production readiness; motivates an absolute expert threshold
